title: caleopsis sarmentosa
intro: Caleopsis sarmentosa, also known as the "Mexican Gold" or "Golden Calea," is a flowering plant native to Mexico and parts of the southwestern United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and alleviating digestive problems. Caleopsis sarmentosa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, making it a popular remedy among herbalists and natural health practitioners.
